acceptance_criteria: The build-pack defines a canonical artifact model for runtime evidence, service inventory, and baseline output.
The first artifact model clearly separates observed facts, inference, and unknowns.
completion_signals: The CLI and source layout expose named surfaces for the first ADF artifact set.
The pack-local objective, backlog, and work-state files all point at the same first implementation slice.
escalation_conditions: The artifact model cannot stay small enough for the MVP without reopening product scope.
The required output set starts to imply broad test or fixture expansion.
tasks.summary: Define the first bounded ADF artifact model for runtime evidence, service inventory, and baseline output.

acceptance_criteria: The build-pack can collect a safe first-pass AlgoSec runtime snapshot from the lab appliance.
The first collection path stays read-only and bounded to support-relevant evidence classes.
completion_signals: A runtime collection command exists for the first ADF slice.
The collection output includes service inventory and core platform checkpoints.
escalation_conditions: The target appliance requires risky write actions or unsupported privilege changes to gather the first evidence set.
The first-pass evidence set expands beyond the MVP boundary of useful support diagnostics.
tasks.summary: Implement the first safe runtime evidence collection path for the controlled AlgoSec lab appliance.

acceptance_criteria: The build-pack renders machine-readable baseline artifacts into a simple support-facing HTML view.
The HTML output remains a render of canonical JSON artifacts rather than a separate source of truth.
completion_signals: The first HTML-ready baseline output exists for the AlgoSec lab path.
Support-facing output is generated from the same artifact model used for machine-readable evidence.
escalation_conditions: The HTML layer starts to dominate the scope instead of remaining a thin render of machine-readable output.
The support-facing output requires broad UX work beyond a simple MVP render.
tasks.summary: Generate the first HTML-ready support output from canonical ADF baseline artifacts.

acceptance_criteria: The top support domains use language and sequencing that match how support engineers actually diagnose customer issues.
The highest-value decision playbooks are tuned for operator review without changing the canonical JSON-first model.
completion_signals: Core AFF and Microservice Platform playbooks reflect reviewed support phrasing and decision order.
HTML and JSON outputs remain aligned after the language tuning pass.
escalation_conditions: Requested wording changes imply a broader UX rewrite instead of playbook tuning.
Support guidance starts to require risky write actions instead of read-only diagnostics.
tasks.summary: Tune high-value support playbooks into final operator language and decision flow.

acceptance_criteria: Priority playbooks embed service-status, listener, config, and log commands for more AlgoSec services and dependencies.
Each command remains read-only and includes a healthy signal plus failure interpretation.
completion_signals: The command bundles extend beyond the current top-path checks and cover more service-specific failure paths.
Service ports and config checkpoints are surfaced more explicitly in the operator-facing output.
escalation_conditions: The required command coverage implies broad appliance-specific scripting beyond the MVP boundary.
The next command set would require unsupported or risky write access on the target appliance.
tasks.summary: Expand service-specific diagnostic command coverage for priority services and dependencies.

acceptance_criteria: The next playbook slice deepens dependency-aware checks for important AlgoSec paths without trying to cover every service equally.
The build-pack preserves the current minimal-testing boundary while extending real operator value.
completion_signals: Shared dependencies and deeper failure branches are clearer for the top customer-facing paths.
The next remote review can focus on specific unresolved service paths instead of the whole appliance.
escalation_conditions: The dependency model starts to force full-system reverse engineering instead of bounded support diagnostics.
The next extension would require broad new tests or fixture matrices.
tasks.summary: Deepen dependency-aware playbooks for the highest-value AlgoSec service paths.
